title: Today's AI Brief: AI warfare is already here
summary: AI warfare is here, Anthropic fights the Pentagon, and AI's impact on careers.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for AI warfare is already here

body.text: I'm looking at some big developments in AI, and I think it's time to break them down. AI is changing fast, and it's affecting many areas, from military tech to our jobs.

First off, AI warfare is already here. A recent report from The Verge highlights how AI is being used in military contexts, and it's gotten pretty intense. Anthropic, an AI company, is in a bit of a fight with the Pentagon over the use of autonomous AI in warfare. This is interesting because it shows just how close we are to AI being a major player in conflicts.

Next, have you ever wondered if AI is going to take your job? Wired has a quiz that tries to forecast AI's effects on various careers between now and 2030. It's a pretty fascinating look at how economists and AI experts think the job market might shift.

I'm also seeing that AI's impact is being studied closely by top researchers. They're trying to understand how AI will change the economy and which jobs might be most affected. It's a complex picture, but it's clear that AI is going to change a lot about how we work and live.

My read is that AI is moving fast and touching many parts of our lives. From military tech to our day jobs, AI is becoming more and more integrated. It's a bit wild to think about, but it's also kind of exciting to see where this technology will take us.
